source_pr.title: planner: eliminate top level Sort in subquery
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

fix https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/11881.

This PR eliminates top level Sort in subquery.

Before this PR:
```
mysql> explain select * from t1 where t1.a in (select t2.a from t2 where t2.b = t1.b order by t2.b);
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| id                       | count    | task | operator info                                               |
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| Apply_12                 | 9990.00  | root | semi join, inner:Sort_16, equal:[eq(test.t1.a, test.t2.a)]  |
| ├─TableReader_15         | 9990.00  | root | data:Selection_14                                           |
| │ └─Selection_14         | 9990.00  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t1.a))                                      |
| │   └─TableScan_13       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t1, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
| └─Sort_16                | 9.99     | root | test.t2.b:asc                                               |
|   └─TableReader_20       | 9.99     | root | data:Selection_19                                           |
|     └─Selection_19       | 9.99     | cop  | eq(test.t2.b, test.t1.b), not(isnull(test.t2.a))            |
|       └─TableScan_18     | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t2, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------+
8 rows in set (0.02 sec)

mysql> explain select * from t1 where t1.a in (select t2.a from t2 where t2.b = t1.b order by t1.b);
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| id                       | count    | task | operator info                                                     |
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Apply_11                 | 9990.00  | root | semi join, inner:TableReader_19, equal:[eq(test.t1.a, test.t2.a)] |
| ├─TableReader_14         | 9990.00  | root | data:Selection_13                                                 |
| │ └─Selection_13         | 9990.00  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t1.a))                                            |
| │   └─TableScan_12       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t1, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo       |
| └─TableReader_19         | 9.99     | root | data:Selection_18                                                 |
|   └─Selection_18         | 9.99     | cop  | eq(test.t2.b, test.t1.b), not(isnull(test.t2.a))                  |
|     └─TableScan_17       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t2, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo       |
+--------------------------+----------+------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
7 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

After this PR:
```
mysql> explain select * from t1 where t1.a in (select t2.a from t2 where t2.b = t1.b order by t2.b);
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| id                       | count    | task | operator info                                                                              |
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| HashLeftJoin_11          | 7984.01  | root | semi join, inner:TableReader_17, equal:[eq(test.t1.b, test.t2.b) eq(test.t1.a, test.t2.a)] |
| ├─TableReader_14         | 9980.01  | root | data:Selection_13                                                                          |
| │ └─Selection_13         | 9980.01  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t1.a)), not(isnull(test.t1.b))                                             |
| │   └─TableScan_12       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t1, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                |
| └─TableReader_17         | 9980.01  | root | data:Selection_16                                                                          |
|   └─Selection_16         | 9980.01  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t2.a)), not(isnull(test.t2.b))                                             |
|     └─TableScan_15       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t2, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                |
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
7 rows in set (0.00 sec)

mysql> explain select * from t1 where t1.a in (select t2.a from t2 where t2.b = t1.b order by t1.b);
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| id                       | count    | task | operator info                                                                              |
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| HashLeftJoin_10          | 7984.01  | root | semi join, inner:TableReader_16, equal:[eq(test.t1.b, test.t2.b) eq(test.t1.a, test.t2.a)] |
| ├─TableReader_13         | 9980.01  | root | data:Selection_12                                                                          |
| │ └─Selection_12         | 9980.01  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t1.a)), not(isnull(test.t1.b))                                             |
| │   └─TableScan_11       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t1, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                |
| └─TableReader_16         | 9980.01  | root | data:Selection_15                                                                          |
|   └─Selection_15         | 9980.01  | cop  | not(isnull(test.t2.a)), not(isnull(test.t2.b))                                             |
|     └─TableScan_14       | 10000.00 | cop  | table:t2, range:[-inf,+inf], keep order:false, stats:pseudo                                |
+--------------------------+----------+------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
7 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```
### What is changed and how it works?

In the rule `decorrelate`, this PR matches the top level `Sort`, and eliminate it.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->
 - Unit test

symptoms: sql: "select t1.b from t t1 where t1.b = (select max(t2.a) from t t2 where t1.b=t2.b order by t1.a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select t1.b from t t1 where t1.b = (select max(t2.a) from t t2 where t1.b=t2.b order by t1.a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "select t1.b from t t1 where t1.b = (select max(t2.a) from t t2 where t1.b=t2.b order by t1.a)", (planner/core/logical_plan_test.go)
